<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-calculus-checkpoint" data-default-total="5"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "limits";
    const BANKS = {
      limits: [limitTableQuestion, limitValueQuestion, limitExistsQuestion],
      derivatives: [derivativeMeaningQuestion, tangentSlopeQuestion, derivativeSignQuestion],
      "derivative-rules": [derivativeRuleQuestion, powerRuleQuestion, chainRuleQuestion],
      optimization: [criticalPointQuestion, endpointQuestion, classifyCriticalQuestion],
      integrals: [areaQuestion, integralMeaningQuestion, antiderivativeQuestion],
      "integration-techniques": [techniqueQuestion, substitutionQuestion, partsQuestion],
      "calculus-applications": [applicationToolQuestion, unitsQuestion, motionQuestion],
      "calculus-cumulative-review": [
        limitValueQuestion,
        derivativeRuleQuestion,
        criticalPointQuestion,
        areaQuestion,
        techniqueQuestion,
        applicationToolQuestion,
      ],
    };

    document
      .querySelectorAll(".edu-math-calculus-checkpoint:not([data-ready])")
      .forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-calculus-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Calculus checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 5))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", {
        class: "quiz-count",
        type: "number",
        min: "1",
        max: "20",
        value: String(state.total),
      });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 5));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element(
          "div",
          { class: "quiz-start" },
          element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input),
          button,
        ),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(
          correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`,
          " ",
          question.explanation,
        );
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function limitTableQuestion() {
      const a = int(-4, 4);
      const b = int(-5, 5);
      return question({
        prompt: "What limit does the table suggest?",
        stimulus: `f(x)=x+${b}; x approaches ${a}`,
        answer: String(a + b),
        distractors: [String(a), String(b), String(a - b), "does not exist"],
        explanation: "For a continuous linear function, substitute the approached input.",
      });
    }

    function limitValueQuestion() {
      const a = int(1, 5);
      return question({
        prompt: "Evaluate the removable-hole limit.",
        stimulus: `lim as x→${a} of (x²-${a * a})/(x-${a})`,
        answer: String(2 * a),
        distractors: ["0", String(a), "does not exist", String(-2 * a)],
        explanation: "Factor and cancel before substituting; the simplified expression is x plus the root.",
      });
    }

    function limitExistsQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Does the two-sided limit exist?",
        stimulus: "left-hand limit = 2; right-hand limit = 5",
        answer: "no",
        distractors: ["yes", "only if f(a)=2", "only if f(a)=5"],
        explanation: "A two-sided limit exists only when the one-sided limits agree.",
      });
    }

    function derivativeMeaningQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does a derivative measure?",
        stimulus: "f'(a)",
        answer: "instantaneous rate of change",
        distractors: ["total accumulated area", "average y-value", "x-intercept"],
        explanation: "A derivative is local change at one input.",
      });
    }

    function tangentSlopeQuestion() {
      const a = int(-4, 4);
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the tangent slope for f(x)=x²?",
        stimulus: `x = ${a}`,
        answer: String(2 * a),
        distractors: [String(a * a), String(a), String(2 * a + 1), "0"],
        explanation: "The derivative of x² is 2x.",
      });
    }

    function derivativeSignQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "If f'(a) is positive, what is the graph doing near a?",
        stimulus: "f'(a) > 0",
        answer: "increasing",
        distractors: ["decreasing", "constant area", "undefined"],
        explanation: "Positive tangent slope means the function is increasing locally.",
      });
    }

    function derivativeRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which rule is the best first choice?",
        stimulus: "d/dx [(x²+1)³]",
        answer: "chain rule",
        distractors: ["product rule", "quotient rule", "constant rule"],
        explanation: "The expression is a composition: an outside cube and an inside polynomial.",
      });
    }

    function powerRuleQuestion() {
      const n = int(2, 6);
      return question({
        prompt: "Differentiate using the power rule.",
        stimulus: `x^${n}`,
        answer: `${n}x^${n - 1}`,
        distractors: [`x^${n - 1}`, `${n - 1}x^${n}`, `${n}x^${n}`, "0"],
        explanation: "Bring the exponent down and subtract one from the exponent.",
      });
    }

    function chainRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What factor is easy to forget?",
        stimulus: "d/dx sin(x²)",
        answer: "2x",
        distractors: ["sin(x)", "x²", "1/2"],
        explanation: "The chain rule multiplies by the derivative of the inside function.",
      });
    }

    function criticalPointQuestion() {
      const c = int(-4, 4);
      return question({
        prompt: "Find the critical point.",
        stimulus: `f(x) = (x-${c})² + 1`,
        answer: `x = ${c}`,
        distractors: [`x = ${-c}`, "x = 0", "x = 1"],
        explanation: "The vertex of (x-c)²+1 occurs at x=c, where the derivative is zero.",
      });
    }

    function endpointQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Why check endpoints on a closed interval?",
        stimulus: "absolute maximum/minimum on [a,b]",
        answer: "best value may occur at an endpoint",
        distractors: ["derivatives do not work", "endpoints are always maxima", "to remove units"],
        explanation: "Critical points are not the only candidates on closed intervals.",
      });
    }

    function classifyCriticalQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Classify the critical point from derivative signs.",
        stimulus: "f' changes from + to -",
        answer: "local maximum",
        distractors: ["local minimum", "no change", "vertical asymptote"],
        explanation: "Increasing then decreasing means the function turns downward.",
      });
    }

    function areaQuestion() {
      const h = int(1, 6);
      const w = int(1, 6);
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the area of the rectangle under the constant function?",
        stimulus: `height ${h}, width ${w}`,
        answer: String(h * w),
        distractors: [String(h + w), String(2 * (h + w)), String(h), String(w)],
        explanation: "Rectangle area is height times width.",
      });
    }

    function integralMeaningQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does a definite integral usually represent?",
        stimulus: "∫ from a to b of f(x) dx",
        answer: "accumulation over an interval",
        distractors: ["instantaneous slope", "one input value", "a tangent line"],
        explanation: "A definite integral adds small pieces across an interval.",
      });
    }

    function antiderivativeQuestion() {
      const n = int(1, 4);
      return question({
        prompt: "Find an antiderivative.",
        stimulus: `${n}x^${n - 1}`,
        answer: `x^${n} + C`,
        distractors: [`${n}x^${n} + C`, `x^${n - 1} + C`, `${n - 1}x^${n} + C`],
        explanation: "An antiderivative differentiates back to the original function.",
      });
    }

    function techniqueQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Choose the best integration technique.",
        stimulus: "∫ 2x cos(x²) dx",
        answer: "substitution",
        distractors: ["parts", "partial fractions", "no technique"],
        explanation: "The factor 2x is the derivative of the inside x².",
      });
    }

    function substitutionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Choose u for substitution.",
        stimulus: "∫ 2x cos(x²) dx",
        answer: "u = x²",
        distractors: ["u = 2x", "u = cos(x)", "u = dx"],
        explanation: "Choose the inside expression whose derivative appears nearby.",
      });
    }

    function partsQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which technique fits a product where one factor simplifies when differentiated?",
        stimulus: "∫ x e^x dx",
        answer: "integration by parts",
        distractors: ["direct power rule", "left Riemann sum", "limit law"],
        explanation: "Integration by parts reverses the product rule.",
      });
    }

    function applicationToolQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Choose the calculus tool.",
        stimulus: "total distance from a velocity function",
        answer: "integral",
        distractors: ["derivative", "critical point only", "tangent line"],
        explanation: "Distance accumulates velocity over time.",
      });
    }

    function unitsQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "If position is meters and time is seconds, what are derivative units?",
        stimulus: "s'(t)",
        answer: "meters per second",
        distractors: ["meters squared", "seconds per meter", "meters-second"],
        explanation: "Derivative units are output units divided by input units.",
      });
    }

    function motionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Velocity is the derivative of what quantity?",
        stimulus: "motion model",
        answer: "position",
        distractors: ["acceleration", "area", "profit"],
        explanation: "Velocity is rate of change of position with respect to time.",
      });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return { prompt, stimulus, answer, explanation, options: shuffle([answer, ...distractors]).slice(0, 4) };
    }

    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const groupName = `calc-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
      const form = element("form", { class: "quiz-options", role: "radiogroup" });
      for (const option of options) {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name: groupName });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        form.append(element("label", { class: "quiz-option" }, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      }
      return form;
    }

    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("div", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, element("div", { class: "quiz-math" }, text));
    }

    function element(tag, attributes = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(attributes)) node.setAttribute(key, value);
      node.append(...children.flat());
      return node;
    }

    function choice(values) {
      return values[Math.floor(Math.random() * values.length)];
    }

    function int(min, max) {
      return Math.floor(Math.random() * (max - min + 1)) + min;
    }

    function shuffle(values) {
      const copy = values.slice();
      for (let index = copy.length - 1; index > 0; index -= 1) {
        const swap = Math.floor(Math.random() * (index + 1));
        [copy[index], copy[swap]] = [copy[swap], copy[index]];
      }
      return copy;
    }
  })();
</script>
